spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (document.querySelector, CSSStyleSheet, adoptedStyleSheets) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Effectively no live controls — does not clear the interactivity bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (adopted, built, component, custom, declarative, defined, elements, entirely, four, library, module, modules, multiple, o).

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure / fallback handling shown (4 hits), but the demo skews toward the happy path and doesn't surface most edge cases the spec calls out.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: 2 button rule(s) override `background` without also overriding `color`. The global button rule sets both, so this leaves the text colour inherited from the dark default (CLAUDE.md rule #6).

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "Declarative CSS Modules Scripts are an extension of the existing script-based CSS Module Scripts. They allow for developers to share declarative stylesheets with shadow roots, including declarative sh". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Add live controls and a runtime hook
openQuestions.detail: Page must offer at least a try-it button that invokes the API and shows its return value or visible side effect; current state is closer to a static card.

openQuestions.title: Demo at least one explicit failure or edge case
openQuestions.detail: Spec / chromestatus motivation mentions failure modes worth showing — permission denial, missing-feature, empty input, malformed payload. Add a control or scripted scenario that walks the user through one and shows the resulting error or fallback.

openQuestions.title: Pair every button background override with a colour override
openQuestions.detail: Audit the inline `<style>` block: each `button { background: ... }` (or `.foo-btn { background: ... }`) needs an explicit `color: ...` next to it. CLAUDE.md rule #6 documents the light-on-light invisibility bug this causes.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Declarative CSS Modules Scripts are an extension of the existing script-based CSS Module Scripts. They allow for developers to share declarative stylesheets with shadow roots, including declarative shadow roots. Developers can define inline") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/11687 to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~3996 chars of script across 1 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
